question: A-6. Let \( f(x) \) be an integrable function in \( 0 \leqq x \leqq 1 \) and suppose \( \int_{0}^{1} f(x) d x=0, \int_{0}^{1} x f(x) d x=0, \cdots \), \( \int_{0}^{1} x^{n-1} f(x) d x=0 \) and \( \int_{0}^{1} x^{n} f(x) d x=1 \). Show that \( |f(x)| \geqq 2^{n}(n+1) \) in a set of positive measure.
solution:
A-6 The conditions imply \( \int_{0}^{1}\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{n} f(x) d x=1 \). Suppose \( |f(x)|<2^{n}(n+1) \) except for a set of measure 0 .

Then \( 1=\int_{0}^{1}\left(x-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{n} f(x) d x<2^{n}(n+1) \int_{0}^{1}\left|x-\frac{1}{2}\right|^{n} d x=1 \), a contradiction.

descriptive_long.question: A-6. Let \( function(variable) \) be an integrable function in \( 0 \leqq variable \leqq 1 \) and suppose \( \int_{0}^{1} function(variable) d variable=0, \int_{0}^{1} variable\, function(variable) d variable=0, \cdots \), \( \int_{0}^{1} variable^{degree-1} function(variable) d variable=0 \) and \( \int_{0}^{1} variable^{degree} function(variable) d variable=1 \). Show that \( |function(variable)| \geqq 2^{degree}(degree+1) \) in a set of positive measure.
descriptive_long.solution:
A-6 The conditions imply \( \int_{0}^{1}\left(variable-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{degree} function(variable) d variable=1 \). Suppose \( |function(variable)|<2^{degree}(degree+1) \) except for a set of measure 0 .

Then \( 1=\int_{0}^{1}\left(variable-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{degree} function(variable) d variable<2^{degree}(degree+1) \int_{0}^{1}\left|variable-\frac{1}{2}\right|^{degree} d variable=1 \), a contradiction.

descriptive_long_confusing.question: A-6. Let \( candlewax(marzipans) \) be an integrable function in \( 0 \leqq marzipans \leqq 1 \) and suppose \( \int_{0}^{1} candlewax(marzipans) d marzipans=0, \int_{0}^{1} marzipans candlewax(marzipans) d marzipans=0, \cdots \), \( \int_{0}^{1} marzipans^{shoelaces-1} candlewax(marzipans) d marzipans=0 \) and \( \int_{0}^{1} marzipans^{shoelaces} candlewax(marzipans) d marzipans=1 \). Show that \( |candlewax(marzipans)| \geqq 2^{shoelaces}(shoelaces+1) \) in a set of positive measure.
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
A-6 The conditions imply \( \int_{0}^{1}\left(marzipans-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{shoelaces} candlewax(marzipans) d marzipans=1 \). Suppose \( |candlewax(marzipans)|<2^{shoelaces}(shoelaces+1) \) except for a set of measure 0 .

Then \( 1=\int_{0}^{1}\left(marzipans-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{shoelaces} candlewax(marzipans) d marzipans<2^{shoelaces}(shoelaces+1) \int_{0}^{1}\left|marzipans-\frac{1}{2}\right|^{shoelaces} d marzipans=1 \), a contradiction.

descriptive_long_misleading.question: A-6. Let \( nonfunction(constantval) \) be an integrable function in \( 0 \leqq constantval \leqq 1 \) and suppose \( \int_{0}^{1} nonfunction(constantval) d constantval=0, \int_{0}^{1} constantval nonfunction(constantval) d constantval=0, \cdots \), \( \int_{0}^{1} constantval^{continuous-1} nonfunction(constantval) d constantval=0 \) and \( \int_{0}^{1} constantval^{continuous} nonfunction(constantval) d constantval=1 \). Show that \( |nonfunction(constantval)| \geqq 2^{continuous}(continuous+1) \) in a set of positive measure.
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
A-6 The conditions imply \( \int_{0}^{1}\left(constantval-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{continuous} nonfunction(constantval) d constantval=1 \). Suppose \( |nonfunction(constantval)|<2^{continuous}(continuous+1) \) except for a set of measure 0 .

Then \( 1=\int_{0}^{1}\left(constantval-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{continuous} nonfunction(constantval) d constantval<2^{continuous}(continuous+1) \int_{0}^{1}\left|constantval-\frac{1}{2}\right|^{continuous} d constantval=1 \), a contradiction.

garbled_string.question: A-6. Let \( hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp) \) be an integrable function in \( 0 \leqq qzxwvtnp \leqq 1 \) and suppose \( \int_{0}^{1} hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp) d qzxwvtnp=0, \int_{0}^{1} qzxwvtnp hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp) d qzxwvtnp=0, \cdots \), \( \int_{0}^{1} qzxwvtnp^{rckpldvu-1} hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp) d qzxwvtnp=0 \) and \( \int_{0}^{1} qzxwvtnp^{rckpldvu} hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp) d qzxwvtnp=1 \). Show that \( |hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp)| \geqq 2^{rckpldvu}(rckpldvu+1) \) in a set of positive measure.
garbled_string.solution:
A-6 The conditions imply \( \int_{0}^{1}\left(qzxwvtnp-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{rckpldvu} hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp) d qzxwvtnp=1 \). Suppose \( |hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp)|<2^{rckpldvu}(rckpldvu+1) \) except for a set of measure 0 .

Then \( 1=\int_{0}^{1}\left(qzxwvtnp-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{rckpldvu} hjgrksla(qzxwvtnp) d qzxwvtnp<2^{rckpldvu}(rckpldvu+1) \int_{0}^{1}\left|qzxwvtnp-\frac{1}{2}\right|^{rckpldvu} d qzxwvtnp=1 \), a contradiction.

kernel_variant.question:
Let $d\ge 2$ and $n\ge 1$ be fixed integers.  
Let  
\[
f:[0,1]^{d}\longrightarrow\mathbb R
\]
be an integrable function that satisfies the moment conditions  

\[
\int_{[0,1]^{d}}x_{1}^{\alpha_{1}}\cdots x_{d}^{\alpha_{d}}\,
      f(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})\,
      dx_{1}\dots dx_{d}=0
\qquad\text{for every multi-index }\alpha=(\alpha_{1},\dots ,\alpha_{d})
\text{ with }|\alpha|:=\alpha_{1}+\dots +\alpha_{d}\le nd-1,
\]

\[
\int_{[0,1]^{d}}x_{1}^{n}\,x_{2}^{n}\dots x_{d}^{n}\,
      f(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})\,
      dx_{1}\dots dx_{d}=1 .
\]

Prove that the set  
\[
E_{\ast}:=\bigl\{(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})\in[0,1]^{d}:
            \,|f(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})|\ge 2^{nd}(n+1)^{d}\bigr\}
\]
has strictly positive Lebesgue measure.  In particular,  

\[
\operatorname*{ess\,sup}_{(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})\in[0,1]^{d}}
      |f(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})|
      \;\ge\;2^{nd}(n+1)^{d}.
\]
kernel_variant.solution:
Step 1 - A degree-$nd$ test polynomial whose integral against $f$ equals $1$.  
Define  
\[
P(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d}):=\prod_{i=1}^{d}\bigl(x_{i}-\tfrac12\bigr)^{n}.
\tag{1}
\]
Expanding one factor yields  
\[
\bigl(x_{i}-\tfrac12\bigr)^{n}=\sum_{k=0}^{n}\binom{n}{k}
      \bigl(-\tfrac12\bigr)^{\,n-k}x_{i}^{k}.
\tag{2}
\]
Hence $P$ is a linear combination of monomials
$x_{1}^{k_{1}}\cdots x_{d}^{k_{d}}$ with total degree
$k_{1}+\dots +k_{d}\le nd$.  
The coefficient of the unique top-degree monomial
$x_{1}^{n}\dots x_{d}^{n}$ is $1$ (every factor contributes the $x_{i}^{n}$-term
with coefficient $1$ and any other choice lowers at least one exponent).

Applying the hypotheses to the expansion of $P$ gives  
\[
\int_{[0,1]^{d}}P\,f
      =\int_{[0,1]^{d}}x_{1}^{n}\dots x_{d}^{n}\,f
      =1,
\tag{3}
\]
because all lower-degree terms vanish by the first moment condition.

Step 2 - The $L^{1}$-norm of $P$.  
By Fubini and symmetry,
\[
\int_{[0,1]^{d}}|P|
    =\prod_{i=1}^{d}\int_{0}^{1}
        \bigl|x_{i}-\tfrac12\bigr|^{n}\,dx_{i}.
\tag{4}
\]
For a single factor,
\[
\int_{0}^{1}\bigl|x-\tfrac12\bigr|^{n}\,dx
    =2\int_{0}^{1/2}\bigl(\tfrac12-x\bigr)^{n}\,dx
    =\frac1{2^{\,n}(n+1)}.
\tag{5}
\]
Taking the product of $d$ identical factors,
\[
\int_{[0,1]^{d}}|P|
      =\Bigl(\frac1{2^{\,n}(n+1)}\Bigr)^{d}
      =\frac1{2^{\,nd}(n+1)^{d}}.
\tag{6}
\]

Step 3 - Assuming $|f|$ is too small leads to a contradiction.  
Suppose, contrary to what we want to prove, that  
\[
|f(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})|<2^{\,nd}(n+1)^{d}
\quad\text{for almost every }(x_{1},\dots ,x_{d})\in[0,1]^{d}.
\tag{7}
\]
Then by Holder's inequality (here, plain absolute convergence suffices) and
equations (3)-(6),
\[
1=\Bigl|\int_{[0,1]^{d}}P\,f\Bigr|
  \le \int_{[0,1]^{d}}|P|\,|f|
  < 2^{\,nd}(n+1)^{d}\cdot\frac1{2^{\,nd}(n+1)^{d}}
  =1,
\]
a contradiction.  Hence assumption (7) fails.

Step 4 - Positivity of the critical level set.  
Since (7) is false, the complement of the set  
\[
E_{\ast}:=\bigl\{x\in[0,1]^{d}:\,
        |f(x)|\ge 2^{\,nd}(n+1)^{d}\bigr\}
\]
cannot have full measure.  Therefore $\mu(E_{\ast})>0$, which is exactly the
desired statement.  In particular,
\[
\operatorname*{ess\,sup}_{x\in[0,1]^{d}}|f(x)|
      \ge 2^{\,nd}(n+1)^{d}.
\]

metadata.difficulty_analysis:
• Dimension jump:  The problem moves from a single interval to a d-dimensional cube, requiring multi–variable integration, multi-indices and Fubini.  
• Exponent explosion:  Instead of vanishing moments up to degree n–1, the function must annihilate every monomial of total degree ≤ nd−1; the number of separate constraints grows combinatorially with both n and d.  
• High-degree target polynomial:  The crucial polynomial P now has degree nd and depends on every coordinate, so computing its L¹–norm forces the solver to separate variables, handle products, and evaluate Beta–function type integrals.  
• Sharper constant:  The required lower bound is 2^{nd}(n+1)^{d}, exponentially larger than in the original, and its derivation hinges on keeping track of dimension-dependent factors.  
• Same core idea, deeper execution:  While the contradiction principle mirrors the original, carrying it out demands considerably more bookkeeping, higher-dimensional reasoning, and comfort with multi-index notation and product measures.

original_kernel_variant.question:

Step 4 - Positivity of the critical level set.  
Since (7) is false, the complement of the set
